output_data: GPU-processed images and measurement tables, returned to CPU memory
use_when: You need extensive GPU-accelerated operations beyond the base CLIJ set, want 300+ GPU operations, or use the CLIJ2 scripting API
do_not_use_when: No compatible GPU; images are small and CPU speed is adequate; operations not in CLIJ2
typical_use_cases: Fast 3D Gaussian blur and background subtraction on large stacks
GPU-accelerated connected component labeling
Building GPU macro workflows with CLIJ2 scripting
Rapid morphological operations on large images

name: clijx-assistant
description: GUI-based assistant for building GPU-accelerated image processing pipelines using CLIJ2. Provides a drag-and-drop interface to construct and optimize GPU processing workflows without coding.
input_data: Any image data to be processed through a CLIJ2 pipeline
output_data: GPU-processed images from the constructed pipeline
use_when: You want to build GPU-accelerated processing pipelines using a visual assistant interface without writing code
do_not_use_when: You prefer scripting; you don't need GPU acceleration; pipeline is very simple
typical_use_cases: Interactively designing GPU image processing workflows
Optimizing CLIJ2 pipelines with visual feedback

name: apoc
description: Accelerated Pixel and Object Classifiers (APOC). GPU-accelerated pixel and object classifiers using random forests, running on the GPU via CLIJ2. Provides fast interactive segmentation with GPU speed.
input_data: 2D/3D fluorescence images for interactive pixel or object classification
output_data: Classification probability maps, segmented label images at GPU speed
use_when: You want interactive machine learning segmentation (like ilastik) but with GPU acceleration for faster processing of large images
do_not_use_when: No compatible GPU; simple thresholding works; you need deep learning (use StarDist/DeepImageJ)
typical_use_cases: GPU-accelerated pixel classification for large 3D stacks
Fast interactive segmentation with real-time GPU preview
Object classification in label images using GPU

name: BaSiC
description: Background and Shading Correction (BaSiC) for optical microscopy. Retrospectively corrects both spatial shading (uneven illumination) and temporal background bleaching from image data without requiring blank reference images.
input_data: Fluorescence image stacks or timelapse data with uneven illumination or background fluorescence drift
output_data: Shading-corrected and background-subtracted images, flatfield and darkfield correction profiles
use_when: You have uneven illumination (vignetting), background fluorescence that bleaches over time, or need to correct both spatial and temporal background without reference images
do_not_use_when: You have proper flatfield/darkfield calibration images (use standard correction); illumination is already uniform; images are not fluorescence
typical_use_cases: Correcting uneven illumination in widefield fluorescence images
Removing background bleaching in long timelapse experiments
Preprocessing whole-slide scanner images for uniform analysis
Retrospective correction of high-content screening data

name: ModularImageAnalysis (MIA)
description: Modular, scripted image analysis framework for building automated workflows in Fiji. Provides a library of modules for image processing, object detection, measurement, and data export that can be assembled into pipelines.
input_data: Any 2D/3D/timelapse image data processable by Fiji modules
output_data: Processed images, measurements tables, exported results in configurable formats
use_when: You need a modular, reproducible image analysis pipeline with a library of pre-built modules, or want to build automated batch processing workflows
do_not_use_when: Simple single-operation analysis; you prefer visual graphical pipelines (use JIPipe); scripting in macro/Jython is preferred
typical_use_cases: Building automated cell analysis pipelines with detection and measurement
Creating reproducible workflows for high-content screening
Modular pipeline development with a library of operations

name: Tissue Analyzer
description: Plugin for automated segmentation and analysis of epithelial tissues. Detects and segments cells in fluorescent images of epithelial monolayers and measures cell shapes, sizes, and topological properties.
input_data: 2D fluorescence images of epithelial cell membranes or junctions (e.g., E-cadherin, ZO-1 staining)
output_data: Segmented cell boundaries, cell shape measurements (area, perimeter, eccentricity), neighbor relationships
use_when: You need to segment and analyze cells in epithelial tissue images, measure cell shape statistics, or study tissue topology
do_not_use_when: Cells are not epithelial/packed; images are 3D or timelapse; you need instance segmentation of isolated cells
typical_use_cases: Quantifying cell packing in Drosophila wing epithelium
Measuring cell shape changes during tissue morphogenesis
Analyzing polygon statistics of epithelial cell sheets

name: PTBIOP
description: Collection of plugins and scripts from the BioImaging and Optics Platform (BIOP) at EPFL. Includes tools for microscopy data management, analysis automation, QuPath bridges, and various utility functions for core facilities.
input_data: Varied microscopy images; specific tools depend on the workflow used
output_data: Analysis results, converted files, quality metrics depending on selected tools
use_when: You need EPFL BIOP-developed tools for microscopy data handling, QuPath integration, or core facility automation workflows
do_not_use_when: You need a specific single-purpose plugin; BIOP tools are not relevant to your workflow
typical_use_cases: Automating image analysis at a microscopy core facility
Bridging Fiji and QuPath for pathology workflows
Using BIOP utility macros for data management

name: Zeiss Quick Start Reader
description: Plugin from EPFL BIOP for quickly reading Zeiss CZI files in Fiji with simplified metadata handling. Provides faster loading of CZI files compared to the full Bio-Formats reader for certain workflows.
input_data: Zeiss CZI microscopy image files
output_data: ImagePlus with image data from CZI file
use_when: You need a quick way to open Zeiss CZI files in Fiji, especially for batch processing where full Bio-Formats metadata is not required
do_not_use_when: You need full metadata extraction (use Bio-Formats); files are not CZI format
typical_use_cases: Batch opening Zeiss CZI files for automated analysis
Quick preview of CZI datasets without full metadata loading

name: MIST
description: Microscopy Image Stitching Tool (MIST) from NIST. Provides accurate and reproducible 2D image stitching for tiled microscopy acquisitions using phase correlation with statistical validation.
input_data: Set of overlapping 2D microscopy tile images with known or estimated grid layout
output_data: Stitched full-field image, translation vectors, confidence statistics per tile
use_when: You need accurate 2D tile stitching with statistical validation of overlap translations, or reproducible stitching for publication-quality results
do_not_use_when: Data is 3D multi-view (use BigStitcher); tiles have no overlap; simple grid stitching suffices (use Grid/Collection Stitching plugin)
typical_use_cases: Stitching large tiled fluorescence scans of tissue sections
Creating whole-slide overview images from tiled confocal data
Reproducible stitching pipelines for high-content imaging

name: SIMcheck
description: Quality control plugin for Structured Illumination Microscopy (SIM) images. Checks raw SIM data and reconstructions for artifacts, bleaching, noise, and reconstruction quality.
input_data: Raw SIM acquisition data (multiple phase/angle images) and/or reconstructed SIM images
output_data: Quality metrics, artifact maps, SNR estimates, Fourier plots for SIM data assessment
use_when: You need to assess the quality of SIM raw data or reconstructions, detect reconstruction artifacts, or validate SIM super-resolution images
do_not_use_when: Images are not from structured illumination microscopy; you need quantitative measurements (not QC)
typical_use_cases: Checking SIM raw data quality before reconstruction
Detecting reconstruction artifacts in SIM images
Optimizing SIM acquisition parameters
Validating SIM super-resolution results

name: NucleusJ
description: Plugin for quantifying 3D nuclear morphology and chromatin organization. Segments nuclei in 3D confocal images and measures nuclear shape, volume, and internal chromatin distribution parameters.
input_data: 3D confocal fluorescence images of stained nuclei (DAPI or similar)
output_data: Segmented nuclei as 3D label images, Results table with nuclear volume, surface area, sphericity, chromatin compaction metrics
use_when: You need to quantify 3D nuclear shape parameters and chromatin distribution in confocal z-stacks
do_not_use_when: Images are 2D (use particle analysis); you need tracking; nuclei are not the primary object of interest
typical_use_cases: Measuring nuclear volume and shape in different cell types
Quantifying chromatin compaction changes during differentiation
Comparing nuclear morphology between conditions

name: MiToBo
description: Microscope Image Analysis Toolbox. Collection of algorithms for microscopy image analysis including cell detection, segmentation, shape analysis, and neuron morphology tools.
input_data: 2D/3D fluorescence microscopy images for cell or neuron analysis
output_data: Detected cells, shape measurements, neuron morphology parameters
use_when: You need MiToBo's specific algorithms for cell detection, neuron morphology, or shape analysis
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji analysis tools suffice; you need deep-learning-based detection
typical_use_cases: Detecting and measuring cell shapes in fluorescence images
Analyzing neuron morphology from microscopy data
Batch processing with MiToBo pipeline tools

name: BigDataProcessor
description: Tool for inspection and processing of very large (TB-scale) image datasets. Built on ImgLib2 and BigDataViewer, allows lazy loading and processing of huge images without loading them fully into memory.
input_data: Very large microscopy image files on disk (HDF5, N5, TIFF stacks) too big to fit in RAM
output_data: Processed image data written back to disk, BigDataViewer visualization
use_when: You need to process images that are too large to load into memory (multi-GB or TB), want to convert formats, or need out-of-core image processing
do_not_use_when: Images fit in memory; you need standard in-memory processing; data is not from supported formats
typical_use_cases: Processing terabyte-scale EM datasets
Converting large image stacks to HDF5/N5 format
Cropping, binning, or applying filters to huge images without loading them fully

name: Morphology (Landini)
description: Collection of morphological image analysis plugins by Gabriel Landini, including advanced binary operations, color segmentation, edge detection, and mathematical morphology tools not in standard ImageJ.
input_data: Binary, grayscale, or color 2D images
output_data: Morphologically processed images, edge images, segmented regions
use_when: You need advanced morphological operations not available in standard ImageJ, such as granulometry, top-hat transforms, or color-based segmentation
do_not_use_when: Standard MorphoLibJ or built-in ImageJ morphological tools are sufficient
typical_use_cases: Advanced binary morphological operations
Color-based image segmentation
Granulometry analysis of textures

name: CMTK Registration
description: Fiji wrapper for the Computational Morphometry Toolkit (CMTK) for brain image registration. Primarily used for Drosophila brain registration to standard atlases.
input_data: 3D fluorescence images of Drosophila brains or similar anatomical structures for atlas registration
output_data: Registered brain images aligned to standard atlas space, transformation parameters
use_when: You need to register Drosophila or similar brain images to a standard atlas for comparative neuroanatomy
do_not_use_when: Images are not neuroanatomy data; you need general-purpose deformable registration (use ElastixWrapper or bUnwarpJ)
typical_use_cases: Registering confocal Drosophila brain images to JRC2018 atlas
Comparing neuron morphologies across specimens
Mapping neuron locations to atlas coordinates

name: Mastodon-Tomancak
description: Tomancak lab extensions for Mastodon tracking. Adds additional analysis tools and custom detectors for tracking cells in Drosophila and zebrafish development datasets.
input_data: 3D+time datasets of developing Drosophila or zebrafish embryos in Mastodon format
output_data: Extended tracking analysis results, developmental lineage data
use_when: You are working with Tomancak lab-style datasets or need their specific Mastodon extensions for Drosophila/zebrafish tracking
do_not_use_when: You are not working with Tomancak lab data formats or similar model organisms
typical_use_cases: Tracking cells in Drosophila wing disc development
Analyzing Drosophila gastrulation cell movements

name: BACMMAN
description: BACteria in Mother Machine ANalyzer. High-throughput analysis plugin for mother machine microfluidics experiments. Automatically detects and tracks bacteria across many channels over time.
input_data: Time-lapse fluorescence images from mother machine microfluidic devices with bacteria-filled channels
output_data: Segmented and tracked bacteria per channel, growth rate measurements, lineage trees, fluorescence quantification
use_when: You have mother machine microfluidic data and need automated bacterial tracking, growth rate measurement, and lineage reconstruction
do_not_use_when: Bacteria are not in mother machine geometry; you have planktonic or biofilm data; cells are mammalian
typical_use_cases: Automated E. coli tracking in mother machine experiments
Measuring single-cell growth rates over generations
Analyzing gene expression dynamics in bacteria lineages
High-throughput antibiotic response studies

name: Mars
description: Single-molecule analysis in real-space (Mars). Analysis platform for single-molecule TIRF and force spectroscopy data. Provides tools for molecule tracking, intensity analysis, and kinetics from smFRET or single-molecule experiments.
input_data: Single-molecule fluorescence microscopy data (TIRF, smFRET, FRET efficiency recordings) or force spectroscopy traces
output_data: Molecule tables with positions, intensities, step detection results, kinetic parameters
use_when: You have single-molecule TIRF or force spectroscopy data and need specialized analysis including step detection, intensity quantification, and kinetics
do_not_use_when: Data is not from single-molecule experiments; you need bulk ensemble measurements
typical_use_cases: Analyzing smFRET efficiency distributions from TIRF movies
Step detection in single-molecule force spectroscopy traces
Quantifying single molecule dwell times and binding kinetics

name: ImagingFCS
description: Fluorescence Correlation Spectroscopy (FCS) analysis plugin. Performs spatial and temporal FCS, FCCS, and imaging FCS analysis directly on fluorescence microscopy image stacks.
input_data: Fluorescence microscopy image stacks acquired for FCS (fast timelapse, typically TIRF or confocal point measurements)
output_data: Autocorrelation curves, diffusion coefficients maps, flow velocity maps, FCS parameter images
use_when: You perform imaging FCS experiments and need to compute correlation functions, diffusion maps, or flow measurements from fluorescence image stacks
do_not_use_when: Data is not FCS-type (too slow acquisition, not point measurements); you need single-particle tracking instead
typical_use_cases: Measuring diffusion coefficient maps of membrane proteins with imaging FCS
Dual-color FCCS analysis of protein interactions
Flow measurements in microfluidic channels via FCS

name: Ultrack
description: Large-scale cell tracking framework using graph-based optimization. Supports tracking with overlapping candidate segmentations and handles cell divisions, merging, and complex topologies.
input_data: Candidate segmentation masks (potentially overlapping) for each timepoint of a timelapse dataset
output_data: Optimized cell tracks, lineage trees, tracking statistics
use_when: You have segmentation-based tracking needs with complex topologies, cell divisions, or ambiguous detections requiring global optimization
do_not_use_when: Simple nearest-neighbor linking works; you need real-time tracking; data has very few objects
typical_use_cases: Tracking densely packed cells with division events
Handling ambiguous detection with overlapping segmentation candidates
Large-scale tracking of embryo development

name: Multi-Template-Matching
description: Plugin for detecting multiple objects in images using template matching. Allows searching for multiple occurrences of a template pattern in an image with non-maximum suppression to avoid duplicate detections.
input_data: 2D grayscale or RGB images containing repeated objects, and a template image of one object
output_data: Detected locations (ROIs or table of coordinates) with correlation scores
use_when: You need to find multiple occurrences of a known object template in images, especially when objects have a consistent appearance
do_not_use_when: Objects have highly variable appearance; you need scale/rotation invariant detection; objects overlap significantly
typical_use_cases: Detecting multiple cells of known shape in a field of view
Locating repeated structures (wells, spots) in microscopy images
Finding all occurrences of a reference particle shape

name: 3D ImageJ Suite
description: A suite of plugins providing enhanced 3D capabilities in ImageJ.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
use_when: You need 3D ImageJ Suite's specific functionality for your image analysis task
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying 3D ImageJ Suite tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows

name: 3Dscript
description: 3Dscript is a plugin animating 3D/4D microscopy data using a natural-language-based syntax.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
use_when: You need 3Dscript's specific functionality for your image analysis task
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying 3Dscript tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows

name: 4d-Tools
description: 4d-Tools is a set of macros for importing legacy 4D microscopy data and Imaris and Micro-Manager datasets. Importing .MP4 and .MOV requires Stanislav Chizhik's FFmpegVideoImportExport plugins. Also includes a highly experimental Hyperstack Projector plugin.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
use_when: You need 4d-Tools's specific functionality for your image analysis task
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying 4d-Tools tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows

name: ActogramJ
description: ActogramJ is a plugin for analyzing actograms.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying ActogramJ tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows

name: AdipoQ
description: ImageJ plugins for quantifying adipocyte morphology and function in tissues and in vitro. For more details, a link to the corresponding publication, and information on how to cite the plugins visit the AdipoQ github page
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
use_when: You need AdipoQ's specific functionality for your image analysis task
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying AdipoQ tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows

description: A collection of macros to study cilia.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

name: AMPT
description: The Aquatic Mammal Photogrammetry Tool (AMPT) is an ImageJ plug-in that simplifies the process of measuring aquatic mammals from aerial imagery. The tool may be configured to support different guided measurements.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

name: Animove
description: A plugin that enables users to process large amounts of video data with low-spec hardware, specifically to quantify and analyze zebrafish kinetic activity with a diversity of parameters and outputs.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

name: AxoNet
description: An implementation of the AxoNet tool which can be used to count axons in light micrographs of optic nerves.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function

typical_use_cases: Applying AxoNet tools to fluorescence microscopy data

description: Enables deep learning for BACMMAN (CPU). Currently using Tensorflow 2x
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

typical_use_cases: Applying BACMMAN-DL tools to fluorescence microscopy data

description: Enables deep learning for BACMMAN (GPU). Currently using Tensorflow 2x
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used

name: BAR
description: BAR (_Broadly Applicable Routines_) is a GitHub-curated collection of scripts that simplifies the daily usage of ImageJ. The collection is focused on Data Analysis, Image Annotation and Segmentation.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
